Doctor_English: Is the symptom purely physical, or do you think there is a psychological or emotional component to it?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo gbà pé àárẹ̀ mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tó ṣeni lẹ́nu ní iṣẹ́. Ó ṣeéṣe pé wọ́n ní ibatan. Ṣùgbọ́n irora naa jẹ gidi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient insight into psychosomatic contribution to fatigue following a workplace trauma, demonstrating health literacy and readiness for integrated biopsychosocial management, requiring both physical investigation to exclude organic disease and psychological therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty walking in a straight line or feeling like the floor is moving?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń rìn bíi ẹni tó mu ọtí nígbà-ìgbà. Ìlẹ̀ ń dà bíi pé ó ń rọ. Ó le ní ìgbà alẹ́ àti nígbà tó dudu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Oscillopsia with disequilibrium worsening in darkness consistent with bilateral vestibular failure or posterior column dysfunction requiring B12 level and MRI.

Doctor_English: Does your household use mosquito nets, and do you sleep under one every night?
Patient_Yoruba: A ní àwọ̀ oṣupa kan ṣoṣo nínú ilé wa fún ọmọ ikoko. Àwọn àgbàlagbà kò lo àwọ̀ oṣupa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Single household insecticide-treated bed net reserved for the infant with no adult usage, leaving other household members unprotected against malaria transmission in a high-endemicity area.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any frothiness or foaming in your urine when you pass it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi máa ń ṣẹ̀dá fèrèsé lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ nígbà tí ó bá kọlu omi nínú ìgbọ̀nsẹ̀. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent foamy urine over two months indicating significant proteinuria, a hallmark of glomerular disease such as nephrotic syndrome, diabetic nephropathy, or IgA nephropathy requiring urine protein quantification.

Doctor_English: Does the rash itch, burn, or cause no sensation at all?
Patient_Yoruba: Àpá náà kò ń jẹ mí ín-ín rárá, kò ń jó, kò sì ń ṣe mí ìrora. Ó ń dún nìkan nígbà tí a bá tẹ̀ ẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: An anaesthetic rash with tenderness only on direct pressure and no spontaneous pruritus or burning, raising significant concern for leprosy given the hypoaesthesia pattern.

Doctor_English: Have you been giving your child undiluted adult medications because you could not find or afford the children's version?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò rí oògùn ọmọ ní ọjà àdúgbò wa. Ìdílé mi sọ pé kí n fún ọmọ mi ní ìdajì tábùlẹ́tì àgbàlagbà. Mo ṣe bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọjọ́ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Two days of adult medication administered at halved doses to a child due to paediatric formulation unavailability, creating significant risk of dosing error, toxicity, or subtherapeutic treatment — a recognised consequence of essential medicines supply chain gaps in Nigeria.

Doctor_English: Have you been receiving any financial support from a government programme or health fund for your treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Kò sí ìrànlọ́wọ́ ìjọba tí mo mọ̀ nípa rẹ̀ fún àrùn mi. Mo ń san owó gbogbo nínú àpò mi. Ó ti fẹ́ pa mi run ní ọrọ̀-ajé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Catastrophic health expenditure without awareness of available government health financing schemes, requiring social work referral and information on NHIS, state indigent programmes, and disease-specific support funds to reduce out-of-pocket expenditure and improve treatment access.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any skin changes such as increased pigmentation in the creases of your hands or in scars?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ìpò ọwọ́ mi ti di dúdú síwájú sí i, pàápàá ní àwọn ìpín. Ọgbẹ́ iṣẹ́ abẹ mi tún ti di dúdú.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hyperpigmentation of the palmar creases and surgical scars indicating increased ACTH drive stimulating melanocyte receptors, the characteristic cutaneous sign of primary adrenal insufficiency requiring an urgent short Synacthen test.

Doctor_English: Have you had a hysteroscopy or laparoscopy before to investigate your fertility or pelvic pain?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ní iṣẹ́ kan tí wọ́n wo ìnú ikùn mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Wọ́n sọ pé wọn rí endometriosis ṣùgbọ́n wọ́n kò ṣe nǹkan kankan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Laparoscopically confirmed endometriosis two years ago without definitive treatment, indicating undertreated pelvic pathology contributing to current fertility challenges, requiring medical or surgical management and fertility specialist referral.

Doctor_English: Is there anything specific you are worried about as your illness progresses, such as pain or being a burden?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo bẹ̀rù irora tó ń bọ̀. Mo tún bẹ̀rù pé ìdílé mi máa ń ṣàánú mi. Mi ò fẹ́ jẹ́ ẹrù fún wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Fear of uncontrolled pain and caregiver burden in a terminally ill patient, common existential concerns in palliative care requiring psychological support, clear communication about symptom management capabilities, and carer wellbeing assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you had any previous admissions for mental health reasons, and how did you find the care you received?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo wọ ilé ìwòsàn ọpọlọ ní ọdún mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn. Ìtọ́jú náà ràn mí lọ́wọ́ ṣùgbọ́n ìrírí ìmọ́kùnlé jẹ mí lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀. Mo ń gbìyànjú láti yẹra fún àtúnwọlé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prior inpatient psychiatric admission experienced as therapeutically helpful but traumatic in terms of restraint or containment experiences, creating a barrier to voluntary admission if needed and requiring trauma-informed care planning for any future episode.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a full health check or MOT done, and do you know all your key health numbers?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò tíì ṣe ìdánwò ìlera pípé rárá. Mi ò mọ̀ ìwọ̀n ẹjẹ̀ mi, suga, tàbí ọ̀rá. Mo jẹ́ ọmọ ọdún àádọ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: No comprehensive health assessment in a fifty-year-old with unknown cardiovascular risk factors, indicating the need for a full health MOT including blood pressure, fasting glucose, lipid profile, BMI, and cancer screening eligibility review.

Doctor_English: Do you have any difficulty with balance when walking, or do you tend to fall to one side?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń yí padà sí apá òsì nígbà tí mo bá ń rìn. Mo ti ṣubú lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta ní ọ̀sẹ̀ kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Left-sided lateropulsion with three falls in one week, indicating ipsilateral cerebellar or vestibular pathology requiring neuroimaging to exclude posterior fossa lesion or cerebrovascular disease.

Doctor_English: How severe is the chest tightness — does it stop you from talking in full sentences or climbing stairs?
Patient_Yoruba: Àyà mi dín mọ́ tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè pari ọ̀rọ̀ kan láìsinmi. Ìtẹ̀lọ́ọ̀dọ̀ kan jẹ ẹni lóru mi. Ó le fún mi ní mẹ́jọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe chest tightness rated eight out of ten causing inability to complete sentences and single-stair limitation, indicating critical cardiorespiratory compromise requiring immediate oxygen, ECG, cardiac biomarkers, and emergency cardiology assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any blood in your phlegm when you cough, even just a small streak, for more than one week?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti rí ẹjẹ̀ pupa nínú ìtọ́ ikọ́ mi fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ó kéré ṣùgbọ́n ó máa ń wáyé ní gbogbo ọjọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Daily haemoptysis for two weeks in a patient with smoking history, a red flag for bronchogenic carcinoma requiring urgent chest CT, sputum cytology, and bronchoscopy regardless of the small volume of blood.

Doctor_English: Can you describe the character of your chest pain — is it crushing, stabbing, or radiating to your arm or jaw?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora náà dájú gan-an, bí ẹni pé wọ́n ń gún mi. Ó ń tan sí apá òsì mi àti ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọrùn mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Patient reports acute, stabbing chest pain with radiation to the left upper extremity and the cervical region.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any difficulty controlling your blood pressure despite taking three medications?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ń mu oògùn ìpàná ẹjẹ̀ mẹ́ta ṣùgbọ́n ẹjẹ̀ mi ṣì ga. Dọ́kítà ti ṣàfikún oògùn méjì ní ọdún méjì. Ó kò dára síwájú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Resistant hypertension on three antihypertensive agents requiring investigation for secondary causes including primary aldosteronism, renal artery stenosis, phaeochromocytoma, and medication non-adherence assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any recurrent urinary infections despite antibiotic treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti gba oògùn àkóràn fún àrùn ito lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rin ní ọdún kan ṣùgbọ́n ó máa ń padà ní ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta. Kò sí ìdánwò àkànṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Recurrent urinary tract infections without structural investigation requiring renal ultrasound, post-void residual, cystoscopy, and urine culture sensitivity patterns to identify predisposing factors.

Doctor_English: How severe is the itching on a scale of one to ten, and does it affect your ability to concentrate?
Patient_Yoruba: Ín-ín mi wà ní ẹjọ lára ọgọ́rùn-ún. Ó ń dẹ́kun iṣẹ́ mi àti ìsùn mi. Mo kò lè jókòó ní ibi ìpàdé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe pruritus rated eight out of ten with occupational and sleep impairment, indicating a significant dermatological or systemic condition requiring urgent investigation including liver function tests, renal function, thyroid profile, and full blood count.

Doctor_English: How many pillows do you need to sleep comfortably, and do you wake up gasping for air?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo nílò ìrọ̀lẹ́ mẹ́ta láti sùn dáradára. Ní àárọ̀ alẹ́, mo máa ń jí dìde tó ń fẹ mí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Orthopnoea requiring three pillows and paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea, classic features of left ventricular failure with pulmonary oedema.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any puffiness around your eyes in the morning, or swelling in your face?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú mi máa ń wú ní àárọ̀ nígbà tí mo bá jí dìde. Ó máa ń rọ ní ọ̀sán. Mo ti rí rẹ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Morning periorbital oedema with diurnal improvement, a presentation consistent with nephrotic syndrome where hypoalbuminaemia causes dependent and facial oedema rather than the purely dependent oedema of cardiac failure.

Doctor_English: How severe is the nausea — have you been unable to keep any food or water down?
Patient_Yoruba: Ríru mi le, mo ń bì gbogbo ohun tí mo bá jẹ tàbí mu. Ko lọ fún ọjọ́ méjì báyìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe nausea with persistent vomiting for two days resulting in inability to tolerate oral intake, raising concern for dehydration and electrolyte imbalance.

Doctor_English: Do you have a regular doctor who knows your full medical history, or do you see different doctors each time?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń rí dọ́kítà tó yàtọ̀ ní ìgbà kọ̀ọ̀kan. Kò sí ẹni tó mọ ìtàn àrùn mi pátápátá. Mo ń bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ ní gbogbo ìpàdé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lack of continuity of care with repeated history-taking from scratch, associated with delayed diagnosis, missed follow-up of chronic conditions, and patient dissatisfaction, highlighting the need for a registered primary care physician relationship.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a painless sore on your genitals that healed on its own, followed later by a rash all over your body?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ kéékèéké kan wà ní ìdì mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn tó sàn fúnra rẹ̀. Lẹ́yìn ọsù méjì, àpá tán gbogbo ara mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Primary syphilitic chancre with spontaneous healing followed by secondary syphilis rash, the classic two-stage progression of Treponema pallidum infection requiring RPR and TPHA serology, with penicillin G treatment and partner notification.

Doctor_English: Have you been working very long hours, and do you feel this is affecting your physical or mental health?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń ṣiṣẹ́ fún wákàtí mẹ́rìnlá ní ọjọ́ kan, ọjọ́ mẹ́fà ní ọ̀sẹ̀. Mo ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí máà gbọ́ àárẹ̀ àti orí fífọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupational overwork of eighty-four hours per week with emerging fatigue and headache, constituting a workplace health risk associated with cardiovascular disease, burnout, and impaired immune function requiring occupational health assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you developed any tightening or thickening of the skin on your hands or face?
Patient_Yoruba: Awọ ara ọwọ́ mi ti di kúnjú àti lile ní oṣù mẹ́fà. Ojú mi tún ti di kúnjú, ẹnu mi sì ti di kéré díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive skin induration affecting the hands and face with microstomia over six months, characteristic features of systemic sclerosis requiring autoantibody testing including anti-Scl-70 and anti-centromere antibodies and organ function assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any change in the size or shape of your abdomen, with visible veins across the surface?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn mi ti pọ̀ sí i àti mo lè rí àwọn iṣan ọ̀nà ẹjẹ̀ tó hàn lórí awọ ara mi. Ìdọ̀tí inu rẹ̀ tún máa ń jẹ mí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Caput medusae with visible dilated abdominal wall veins in a patient with ascites, a pathognomonic sign of portal hypertension secondary to liver cirrhosis requiring urgent hepatology assessment and variceal screening.

Doctor_English: Do you have pain before eating, after eating, or does food actually make the pain better?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora mi máa ń wáyé ṣáájú oúnjẹ, nígbà tí ikùn mi bá ń gbẹ. Jíjẹun máa ń mu irora náà lọ fún ìgbà díẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Epigastric pain relieved by food intake and worsening in the fasting state, a pattern characteristic of duodenal ulcer disease requiring H. pylori testing and acid suppression therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you had diarrhoea with blood or mucus in it, along with cramping abdominal pain and fever?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbẹ́ mi ní ẹjẹ̀ àti omi líle nínú fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta. Irora ikùn tó le ń tẹ̀lé rẹ̀, àti ibà mi tún wà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Dysenteric syndrome with bloody mucoid diarrhoea, colicky abdominal pain, and fever for three days, consistent with invasive enterocolitis from Shigella, Entamoeba histolytica, or Campylobacter, requiring stool microscopy and culture.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing any pains or stiffness that are worse after sitting for long periods?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn jíjókòó fún wákàtí mẹ́fà ní ọfiisi, ẹ̀hin mi àti ìbàdí mi di jáko. Ó máa ń yẹ̀ lẹ́yìn ìrìn kéékèéké. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ọdún kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged sitting-induced lumbar stiffness relieved by movement, consistent with mechanical low back pain and hip flexor tightness requiring ergonomic workstation assessment and physiotherapy.

Doctor_English: On a scale of one to ten, how severe is the burning when you pass urine, and is it getting worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora iná ní ìtọ mi wà ní mẹ́jọ lára ọgọ́rùn-ún. Ó ti ń burú sí i ní gbogbo ọjọ́ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ kan. Ibà tún ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ọjọ́ méjì sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive dysuria rated eight out of ten over one week with recent fever onset, indicating ascending urinary tract infection progressing toward pyelonephritis, requiring urgent urine culture, blood cultures, and empirical antibiotic therapy.

Doctor_English: Have you travelled internationally recently, and did you take any malaria prevention medication?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo padà wá láti Cameroon ní ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá sẹ́yìn. Mi ò mu oògùn ìdáàbòbò fún àrùn ibà. Ẹfọn jẹ mí lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ níbẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Return from a malaria-endemic West African country ten days ago without chemoprophylaxis and significant mosquito exposure, presenting within the incubation period for Plasmodium falciparum malaria and requiring urgent thick and thin blood film examination.

Doctor_English: Have you been to the emergency department more than three times in the past year for the same problem?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ti wọ ilé ìwòsàn ìpàdédè lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́rìnlá ní ọdún kan fún irora orí mi. Wọ́n máa ń tọ́jú mi kí wọ́n sì jí mi lọ. Ko dára sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: High emergency department utilisation for headache without outpatient management plan, indicating a care gap where chronic headache should be managed proactively in primary or neurology care with a personalised emergency action plan rather than repeat acute presentations.

Doctor_English: How long has the patient been unconscious, and was there any seizure activity before they collapsed?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ti sùn àìdáwọ dúró fún ìṣẹ́jú ogún. Ṣáájú ìṣubú rẹ̀, ara rẹ̀ gbọn fún ìṣẹ́jú márùn-ún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged post-ictal unconsciousness of twenty minutes following a five-minute convulsive episode, requiring airway protection, blood glucose measurement, and neurological assessment to exclude status epilepticus or structural brain pathology.

Doctor_English: Has there been any significant weight loss, night sweats, or contact with a person diagnosed with tuberculosis?
Patient_Yoruba: Àárẹ̀ pọ̀ mi ti ń padà kù, mo ń gbọn ní alẹ́, àti pé ìyá àgbà mi ni a ṣẹ̀ṣẹ̀ mú ibà ẹ̀dọ̀fóró jẹ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Notable weight loss, nocturnal diaphoresis, and a history of household contact with a recently diagnosed pulmonary tuberculosis case.

Doctor_English: Do you experience any chest pain when you climb stairs, and does it go away when you stop?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá gùn ìtẹ̀lọ́ọ̀dọ̀, irora kéékèéké ń wáyé ní àyà mi. Nígbà tí mo bá dúró, ó máa ń lọ ní ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Stable exertional angina with reproducible chest pain on stair climbing and relief within three minutes of rest, consistent with fixed coronary artery stenosis requiring stress testing and cardiology referral.

Doctor_English: Can you describe the character of the pain — is it sharp like a knife, burning like fire, or dull like a pressure?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora náà dùn bíi ẹni tó ń gún mi pẹ̀lú abẹ́rẹ̀ tó gún jìnnà. Ko dùn bíi ìtẹ̀wọ́ tàbí iná. Ó ń wáyé lójijì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Sharp stabbing pain with acute onset distinguishing neuropathic or pleuritic aetiology from the pressure-like discomfort of ischaemia or the burning quality of inflammatory conditions, guiding analgesic selection and differential diagnosis.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any swelling or lump in your neck that has been there for more than four weeks?
Patient_Yoruba: Èso kan wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọrùn mi ọ̀tún fún oṣù méjì. Kò ń dùn. Wọ́n fun mi ní oògùn àkóràn ṣùgbọ́n kò lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent cervical lymphadenopathy for two months unresponsive to antibiotic therapy, exceeding the four-week threshold warranting urgent biopsy to exclude lymphoma, metastatic squamous cell carcinoma, or tuberculosis.

Doctor_English: Are there any industries or factories near your home that might be releasing chemicals or smoke?
Patient_Yoruba: Ilé iṣẹ́ tó ń ṣe àwo wà ní ìdẹ́ abúlé wa. Èéfín àti ìrísí àjèjì máa ń wá sí inú ilé wa nígbà tí afẹ́fẹ́ bá ń fẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Proximity to a paint manufacturing facility with reported indoor infiltration of industrial fumes, constituting a significant environmental health hazard with potential exposure to volatile organic compounds, heavy metals, and respiratory irritants.

Doctor_English: Has the affected skin area become thickened or leathery from repeated scratching?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọ ara ní àyíká ìdọ̀tí mi ti di lile àti kíki nítorí mo ti ń gbá a fún oṣù mẹ́ta.
Clinical_Translation_English: Lichenification of the perianal skin secondary to chronic scratching over three months, consistent with lichen simplex chronicus complicating a primary pruritic condition such as eczema or psoriasis.

Doctor_English: Has the problem been there since you had an operation, injection, or procedure done?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora bẹ̀rẹ̀ lẹ́yìn abẹ́rẹ̀ ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ẹ̀hin mi ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Dọ́kítà sọ pé ó jẹ́ abẹ́rẹ̀ àtọ̀gbẹ̀ ẹ̀gbẹ̀ ẹ̀hin. Irora kò lọ rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent pain following an epidural steroid injection two years ago, which may indicate post-procedural complications including epidural haematoma, infection, arachnoiditis, or failure of the underlying disc disease to respond, requiring MRI spine and pain specialist review.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any episodes where you feel your surroundings are unreal or dreamlike?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní ìgbà-ìgbà, ayé ń dà bíi pé kò jẹ́ gidi fún mi. O ń dà bíi àlá. Ó ń ṣẹlẹ̀ ní ìgbà tí inú mi bá ń yọ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Derealisation episodes triggered by anxiety consistent with dissociative symptoms in panic disorder or depersonalisation-derealisation disorder requiring cognitive-behavioural therapy and anxiety management.

Doctor_English: Is the numbness confined to the tips of your fingers, the whole hand, or does it go up your arm?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìmọ̀ ọwọ́ mi kò wà ní àwọn ìlà ọwọ́ mi mẹ́ta, tó ń rọ sínu ọwọ́ mi ṣùgbọ́n kò gùn sínu apá mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Numbness confined to the median nerve distribution of the first three fingers and palm without proximal extension, the anatomical distribution of carpal tunnel syndrome, confirmed by Tinel's and Phalen's tests and nerve conduction studies.

Doctor_English: Have your symptoms changed at all since you first came in today, while waiting to be seen?
Patient_Yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ ni, irora àyà mi ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí tan sí apá òsì mi àti ẹrẹ̀kẹ́ mi ní wákàtí kan kẹ́yìín. Ó le sí i ju ìgbà tí mo dé lọ.
Clinical_Translation_English: Symptom evolution during the waiting period with new radiation of chest pain to the left arm and jaw, constituting progression to a higher-risk ACS presentation requiring immediate triage escalation, ECG, troponin, and emergency cardiology review.

Doctor_English: Have you been having any urinary leakage during intercourse or immediately after?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito máa ń jáde ní ìgbà ìbálòpọ̀ àti lẹ́yìn rẹ̀ ní ìgbà-ìgbà. Ó ń jẹ ẹlẹ́yà mi àti ó ti ń dẹ́kun ìbálòpọ̀ wa.
Clinical_Translation_English: Coital urinary incontinence causing sexual avoidance and relationship strain, indicating stress or mixed urinary incontinence requiring pelvic floor physiotherapy, urodynamic assessment, and, if conservative measures fail, surgical intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you been having episodes of feeling very hot, sweating, and your heart racing at the same time?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìgbóná, ríru ẹjẹ̀, àti ọkàn tó ń lu yára wáyé papọ̀ fún ìṣẹ́jú mẹ́wàá ní ìgbà-ìgbà. O ti ń ṣẹlẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní oṣù kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Episodic sympathetic surge with flushing, diaphoresis, and tachycardia requiring twenty-four hour urinary catecholamines and metanephrines to exclude phaeochromocytoma.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any thickening or scaling of your skin particularly on your legs, associated with itching?
Patient_Yoruba: Awọ ara ẹsẹ̀ mi méjèéjì ti ń gbẹ àti dín kù bíi ẹní. Ín-ín ń ṣe mí, pàápàá ní alẹ́. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọdún méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Bilateral lower limb lichenification with pruritus and ichthyosis-like scaling over two years in a patient from an onchocerciasis-endemic area, features of chronic onchocercal skin disease requiring skin snip examination and ocular assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you been drinking alcohol to help you sleep, and how many units do you have before bed?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń mu ọtí ní alẹ́ láti ràn mí lọ́wọ́ láti sùn. Mo máa ń mu ìkòkò méjì tàbí mẹ́ta ṣáájú ìsùn. O ti ń pọ̀ sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Escalating alcohol use as a sleep aid with two to three bottles nightly, indicating alcohol-dependent insomnia where alcohol disrupts sleep architecture causing rebound insomnia on cessation, perpetuating a dependence cycle requiring CBT-I and graduated alcohol reduction.

Doctor_English: Is the patient breathing on their own, and how fast is the breathing rate?
Patient_Yoruba: Ó ń mí, ṣùgbọ́n mímí rẹ̀ yára gan-an. Mo kà ìgbà mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dọ́gbọ̀n tó mí ní ìṣẹ́jú kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Tachypnoea at thirty-five breaths per minute with preserved spontaneous breathing, indicating significant respiratory compromise requiring immediate oxygen supplementation and investigation for pneumonia, pulmonary embolism, or metabolic acidosis.

Doctor_English: How severe is the abdominal cramp right now, and does pressing on your abdomen make it worse?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora ikùn mi wà ní mẹ́wàá lára ọgọ́rùn-ún lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́. Nígbà tí a bá tẹ ikùn mi, irora náà ń burú lẹ́sẹ̀kẹsẹ̀. Ìdọ̀tí mi tún kò ṣẹlẹ̀ fún ọjọ́ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Maximum severity abdominal pain with rebound tenderness on palpation and absolute constipation, constituting a surgical emergency with peritonitis signs requiring immediate surgical assessment, intravenous access, and urgent operative planning.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your liver is enlarged, and do you know why?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà tẹ ikùn mi ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn, ó sọ pé ẹ̀dọ̀ mi pọ̀ jù. Kò ṣe ìdánwò mọ́. Mi ò mu ọtí rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hepatomegaly without investigation in a non-drinker requiring liver ultrasound, hepatitis B and C serology, and liver function tests to determine the aetiology.

Doctor_English: How severe is your dizziness — does it prevent you from standing, or can you still move around if you are careful?
Patient_Yoruba: Orí mi yípo tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè dúró tàbí rìn. Mo ní láti di ohun kan mú nígbà gbogbo. Ó wà ní mẹ́sàn-án lára ọgọ́rùn-ún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe vertigo rated nine out of ten causing complete inability to ambulate without support, indicating acute vestibular failure, posterior circulation stroke, or Ménière's crisis requiring urgent neurological assessment and fall prevention measures.

Doctor_English: Has the child lost a significant amount of weight recently despite eating normally?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi jẹun dáradára ṣùgbọ́n ó ti tẹ̀nìí ní oṣù méjì. Ó tún máa ń rẹ̀ àti ní ìbà kéékèéké ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Unexplained weight loss in a child despite adequate oral intake with fatigue and low-grade fever, raising concern for malignancy, tuberculosis, inflammatory bowel disease, or undiagnosed diabetes, requiring thorough systemic evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any discharge from your penis or pain when you pass urine, with or without a rash?
Patient_Yoruba: Omi tó ní àwọ̀ pàálu-funfun ń jáde nínú òpòlopò mi ní òwúrọ̀. Ìtọ mi tún ń jó mi ní ìgbà tí mo bá ń ṣe.
Clinical_Translation_English: Purulent urethral discharge with dysuria in a male patient, the classic presentation of gonococcal urethritis caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae, requiring urethral swab and culture alongside chlamydia co-testing.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a family history of a condition that means you should be screened more often?
Patient_Yoruba: Ẹ̀gbọ́n mi àgbàlagbà ni àrùn jẹjẹrẹ ifun ní ọjọ́-orí ogójì. Dọ́kítà kò sọ ohun ti èyí túmọ̀ sí fún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: First-degree relative with colorectal cancer at forty years, classifying the patient as high familial risk requiring colonoscopy from age thirty or ten years before the affected relative's diagnosis, whichever is earlier, with five-yearly surveillance thereafter.

Doctor_English: Have you experienced any pain or lumps in your testicles, and have you ever checked yourself regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí èso kan nínú ìdọ̀tí ọ̀tún mi tí kò ń dùn fún oṣù kan. Mi ò mọ̀ pé àwọn ọkùnrin nílò ṣíṣe ìdánwò ara wọn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Painless testicular lump of one month duration without prior self-examination education, a presentation consistent with testicular malignancy — the most common solid tumour in young men — requiring urgent scrotal ultrasound, tumour markers, and urology referral.

Doctor_English: Have you been experiencing nausea that stops you from eating, and have you vomited frequently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ríru ń dẹ́kun mi láti jẹun fún ọjọ́ mẹ́wàá. Mo ègbọn lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ní ọjọ́ kan. Ara mi ti dín kù gan-an.
Clinical_Translation_English: Intractable nausea with frequent vomiting causing significant weight loss and nutritional deterioration in a palliative patient, requiring antiemetic optimisation, review of opioid-induced nausea, and consideration of subcutaneous medication delivery if oral route is compromised.

Doctor_English: Is the child passing urine normally, and have you noticed any swelling around the eyes in the morning?
Patient_Yoruba: Ojú ọmọ mi máa ń wú ní àárọ̀ owúrọ̀ fún ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì. Ito rẹ̀ tún ti di rórò. Mo ti rí ẹ̀jẹ̀ nínú rẹ̀ lẹ́ẹ̀kan.
Clinical_Translation_English: Periorbital oedema with haematuria in a child constituting the classic presentation of post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis or nephrotic syndrome, requiring urine protein, creatinine, complement levels, and ASOT.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any rough raised patches on your skin around your ankles and shins that are very itchy?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ẹ̀gbọ̀n tó gbẹ àti ga wà ní àyíká ẹsẹ̀ ìsàlẹ̀ mi àti ìhà àtẹ̀lẹ ẹsẹ̀ mi. Ín-ín ń ṣe mí gan-an, pàápàá ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Hyperkeratotic pruritic papules in a gaiter distribution around the ankles and shins, consistent with nodular prurigo or chronic onchocercal skin disease, requiring skin snip examination and consideration of ivermectin therapy.

Doctor_English: Did the difficulty swallowing start with solids first, or were you unable to swallow liquids from the beginning?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohun tó le ni mi ò lè gbé àárọ̀. Ní báyìí, omi tún ti ń jẹ mí nira. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́ta sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive dysphagia commencing with solids three weeks ago and now including liquids, a pattern highly suggestive of oesophageal malignancy or severe stricture requiring urgent endoscopic evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty breathing or feeling like you cannot get enough air, even when you are resting?
Patient_Yoruba: Mí mi kúrú ní ìgbà tí mo bá jókòó àti nígbà tí mo bá dùbúlẹ̀. Ó ń bani lóru púpọ̀. Mo máa ń bẹ̀rù ní alẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Refractory dyspnoea at rest causing significant distress and nocturnal fear in a palliative patient, requiring low-dose opioids for breathlessness palliation, benzodiazepines for anxiety, and a fan directed at the face to stimulate trigeminal airflow receptors.

Doctor_English: On a scale of one to ten, how would you rate the severity of your headache right now?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora orí mi wà ní mẹ́sàn-án lára ọgọ́rùn-ún. Ó le tó jẹ́ kí n máà lè ṣiṣẹ́ rárá. Mo ní ìjọba ojú tún.
Clinical_Translation_English: Severe headache rated nine out of ten with associated photophobia and inability to function, constituting a high-severity presentation requiring urgent exclusion of subarachnoid haemorrhage, meningitis, and hypertensive emergency.

Doctor_English: Has anyone in your family had an aneurysm or a blood vessel that burst in the brain?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyá mi kú lójijì ní ọjọ́-orí àádọ́ta nítorí ẹjẹ̀ ọpọlọ. Dọ́kítà sọ pé àlọ̀ ọpọlọ rẹ̀ ya. Mo ń yọ̀ pé ó lè ṣẹlẹ̀ sí mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: First-degree family history of subarachnoid haemorrhage placing the patient at threefold increased risk, warranting MR angiography screening and neurovascular specialist referral.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any food coming back up into your mouth hours after eating without any nausea?
Patient_Yoruba: Oúnjẹ máa ń padà wá sí ẹnu mi wákàtí kan tàbí méjì lẹ́yìn jíjẹun. Ko ní ìrora tàbí ríru. Ó máa ń jẹ mí ẹnu.
Clinical_Translation_English: Effortless regurgitation of undigested food hours post-prandially without nausea, the characteristic symptom of a pharyngeal pouch (Zenker's diverticulum) or achalasia, requiring barium swallow and upper endoscopy.

Doctor_English: Is there any active bleeding from a wound, and has pressure been applied to control it?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọgbẹ́ tó jinlẹ̀ wà ní ìhà àyà rẹ̀ tí o ń jẹ̀ ẹjẹ̀ púpọ̀. A ti tẹ ọwọ́ mọ́ ọn ṣùgbọ́n ẹjẹ̀ ṣì ń jáde.
Clinical_Translation_English: Deep penetrating chest wound with persistent haemorrhage despite manual pressure application, indicating possible haemothorax or injury to an intercostal vessel requiring emergency surgical review and blood product preparation.

Doctor_English: Where exactly is the headache — is it at the front, the back, or on one side of your head?
Patient_Yoruba: Orí mi ń dùn lẹ́yìn orí mi, tó sì ń tan wá sí ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọrùn mi. Ko wà niwájú rárá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occipital headache with radiation to the cervical region. Anterior cranium uninvolved. Tension-type or cervicogenic headache suspected.

Doctor_English: Do you get any swelling in your abdomen that feels like fluid sloshing around inside?
Patient_Yoruba: Ikùn mi ti wú, nígbà tí mo bá rọ́ sí ẹ̀gbẹ́, mo ń gbọ́ ìró omi. Ó ti ń pọ̀ sí i fún oṣù méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive ascites with shifting dullness on clinical examination over two months, raising concern for hepatic cirrhosis, cardiac failure, or malignant peritoneal disease requiring urgent abdominal ultrasound and diagnostic paracentesis.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any small, painful red bumps or nodules under your skin near your joints?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo rí àwọn èso kéékèéké tó ń dùn ní ìsàlẹ̀ awọ ara mi ní àtẹ̀lẹ ìpín ọwọ́ mi àti ẹ̀hin orúkún mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Subcutaneous nodules at pressure points over joint extensor surfaces, highly characteristic of rheumatoid arthritis with seropositive disease, indicating significant joint damage risk and requiring aggressive disease-modifying antirheumatic drug therapy.

Doctor_English: How long have you had this rash, and did it start in one place before spreading?
Patient_Yoruba: Àpá náà bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ọwọ́ mi ọ̀tún ní ọjọ́ márùn-ún sẹ́yìn, lẹ́yìn náà ó tàn sí àyà mi àti ẹ̀gbẹ́ ọrùn mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: A unilateral upper limb rash of five days duration with centripetal spread to the trunk and neck, consistent with contact dermatitis, herpes zoster, or a viral exanthem requiring dermatological assessment.

Doctor_English: Have you ever had a bad reaction to any medication, including rashes, swelling, or difficulty breathing?
Patient_Yoruba: Oògùn pẹ̀nísílíìnì ṣe mí ní ìgbà tí mo wà lọ́mọdé. Awọ ara mi bò pẹ̀lú àpá, ọfun mi sì wú. A gbé mi wọ ilé ìwòsàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Documented anaphylactic reaction to penicillin in childhood with urticaria and angioedema requiring hospitalisation, constituting a severe drug allergy that must be clearly documented and communicated to preclude all beta-lactam antibiotic use.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have protein in your urine, and have you been investigated for the cause?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé ìtọ mi ní àmì ẹjẹ̀ nínú rẹ̀ ní ọdún kan sẹ́yìn. Ko ṣe ìdánwò mọ́. Mo ní àtọ̀gbẹ̀ fún ọdún mẹ́wàá.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent proteinuria in a ten-year diabetic without follow-up nephrological assessment, indicating diabetic nephropathy requiring ACE inhibitor initiation, tight glycaemic and blood pressure control, and annual urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio monitoring.

Doctor_English: Is the vision loss affecting your ability to see things in the centre or around the edges?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní àárín ojú mi ni ó ń ṣòro jù. Ohun tí mo bá ń wo tàààrà, ó máa ń ṣòòfà tàbí ṣúnkú, gẹ́gẹ́ bí ọmọ aláṣẹ ìwòsàn ṣe ṣàlàyé.
Clinical_Translation_English: Central scotoma with preservation of peripheral vision, consistent with macular pathology such as age-related macular degeneration, macular hole, or central serous chorioretinopathy requiring urgent retinal imaging.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any hoarseness in your voice lasting more than three weeks without a cold?
Patient_Yoruba: Ohùn mi ti yí padà, di bíi ẹni tó ń ṣàìsàn ní ọfun fún ọ̀sẹ̀ mẹ́fà, ṣùgbọ́n kò sí otutu tàbí ikọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Persistent hoarseness of six weeks duration without upper respiratory infection, a red flag symptom for laryngeal carcinoma particularly in tobacco users requiring urgent direct laryngoscopy and ENT assessment.

Doctor_English: Does the pain seem to be in the bone itself, or more in the surrounding muscles and soft tissue?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora wà jíjìn nínú eegun mi, kì í ṣe ní ita rẹ̀. Nígbà tí a bá tẹ eegun tààrà, irora ń burú sí i.
Clinical_Translation_English: Deep bone tenderness on direct palpation distinguishing bony pathology such as stress fracture, osteomyelitis, or malignant infiltration from superficial myofascial pain, requiring X-ray and MRI for suspected bone pathology.

Doctor_English: Has your child been meeting their developmental milestones — sitting, walking, and talking at the expected ages?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi jẹ́ ọmọ ọdún méjì ṣùgbọ́n kò sọ ọ̀rọ̀ kankan rárá. Ó ń rìn ṣùgbọ́n kò dáhùn sí orúkọ rẹ̀ tàbí ìpè.
Clinical_Translation_English: Language delay with absence of any words at age two years and failure to respond to name, raising significant concern for autism spectrum disorder, hearing impairment, or global developmental delay requiring urgent audiological assessment and developmental paediatric evaluation.

Doctor_English: Have you been having skin rashes specifically on your hands that are worse at work and better on weekends?
Patient_Yoruba: Àpá tó ń yọ wà lórí ọwọ́ mi méjèéjì. Ó máa ń burú ní àwọn ọjọ́ ìkọ́lé àti yẹ̀ ní ìsinmi. Mo ń ṣiṣẹ́ ní ọjà ẹja.
Clinical_Translation_English: Occupational contact dermatitis with characteristic work-related exacerbation and weekend improvement in a fish market worker, indicating exposure to fish proteins, preservatives, or cleaning agents requiring patch testing and allergen identification.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any painless swelling in your groin or scrotum that increases when you stand or cough?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìdì mi ọ̀tún ń wú nígbà tí mo bá dúró. Nígbà tí mo bá gbẹ ikọ́, ó pọ̀ sí i. Mo lè fi ọwọ́ tẹ̀ ẹ́ padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Reducible inguinal swelling increasing on Valsalva manoeuvre consistent with an indirect inguinal hernia, requiring surgical assessment to determine elective versus urgent repair and to exclude strangulation risk.

Doctor_English: Have you been told your heart valves are leaking or narrow, and has this been monitored recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà sọ pé àwọ ọkàn mi kan ń jó nígbà tí mo bí ọmọ. Kò sí ìdánwò mìíràn lẹ́yìn ọdún mẹ́fà náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Valvular regurgitation identified six years ago without subsequent echocardiographic surveillance, creating risk of undetected haemodynamic progression to heart failure or indication for surgical valve repair or replacement, requiring immediate echocardiography.

Doctor_English: Can you read the label on your medication bottle, and do you understand the dosing instructions?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò kà ọ̀rọ̀ Gẹ̀ẹ́sì dáradára. Ọmọ mi ni ó máa ń ka àmì oògùn fún mi, ṣùgbọ́n kò wà ní ilé ní gbogbo ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Limited English literacy with dependence on a child for medication label interpretation, creating significant risk for dosing errors when the child is unavailable, and indicating the need for pictorial medication instructions in the patient's preferred language.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a difficult airway or that a previous anaesthetist had trouble putting a breathing tube in?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà àánì ní ilé ìwòsàn mìíràn sọ pé ọ̀nà mímí mi ṣòro. Ó gbìyànjú lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́fà ṣáájú kí ó tó ṣẹ̀ṣẹ̀ ṣaṣeyọrí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Previously documented difficult intubation requiring six attempts, constituting a critical airway alert requiring advance notification to the anaesthetic team, awake fibreoptic intubation planning, and a difficult airway cart at the bedside before any sedation is administered.

Doctor_English: How many weeks pregnant are you, and have you been attending your antenatal clinic regularly?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo loyún oṣù márùn-ún. Mo ti lọ sí ìbẹ̀wò oyún lẹ́ẹ̀mẹ́ta nìkan nítorí owó àti ọkọ àgbàdo tó jìnnà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Primigravida at twenty weeks gestation with only three antenatal visits due to financial constraints and distance to clinic, indicating poor antenatal coverage and elevated risk for undetected obstetric complications.

Doctor_English: Have you been told you have a heart condition that would require antibiotics before dental or surgical procedures?
Patient_Yoruba: Dọ́kítà ọkàn mi sọ pé mo nílò oògùn àkóràn ṣáájú gbogbo iṣẹ́ abẹ nítorí àrùn àwọ ọkàn mi. Mo gbàgbé láti sọ fún dọ́kítà rẹ̀yìí.
Clinical_Translation_English: Undisclosed cardiac valvular disease requiring antibiotic endocarditis prophylaxis before invasive procedures, a critical safety omission that must be addressed before proceeding with any planned dental or surgical intervention.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed that your urine has a very strong sweet smell like nail polish remover?
Patient_Yoruba: Ito mi ní òórùn bíi ọ̀pẹ tàbí olóórun didùn pàtàkì fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta. Mo tún ń ni ríru àti àárẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Acetone-smelling urine with nausea characteristic of diabetic ketoacidosis requiring immediate blood glucose, ketones, and urgent DKA management.

Doctor_English: Do you have a burning sensation in your throat or chest after meals, and does it taste acidic?
Patient_Yoruba: Nígbà tí mo bá jẹun, ìró iná máa ń wáyé nínú àyà mi tó sì ń dé ọfun mi. Itọ́ ekan máa ń wá sí ẹnu mi.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-prandial retrosternal burning with acid regurgitation reaching the pharynx, pathognomonic of gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, worsened by recumbency and large meals.

Doctor_English: Have you heard about any disease outbreaks in your community recently, such as cholera or measles?
Patient_Yoruba: Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi sọ pé àwọn ọmọ díẹ̀ ní abúlé wa ti ní ìbà àti gbuuru ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì sẹ́yìn. Kò sí ìkìlọ̀ fún ilé ìwòsàn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Community reports of febrile diarrhoeal illness in children without formal disease notification two weeks prior, indicating a possible cholera or rotavirus outbreak requiring urgent public health reporting, water source testing, and oral rehydration distribution.

Doctor_English: Do you work in a dusty environment, and have you ever worked in a mine or with asbestos?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo ṣiṣẹ́ ilé iṣẹ́ iyẹ̀ fún ọdún mẹ́wàá. Erùpẹ̀ ń pọ̀ níbẹ̀, a kò sì máa ń wọ ohun ìdáàbòbò.
Clinical_Translation_English: Ten years of occupational exposure to flour dust without respiratory protective equipment, raising concern for occupational asthma, hypersensitivity pneumonitis, or baker's lung disease.

Doctor_English: Have you had episodes of sudden severe joint pain, redness, and swelling that resolved within a week?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora tó le, pupa, àti wiwú wáyé ní ìpín ọwọ́ orùka mi lójijì ní alẹ́. Ó parí ní ọjọ́ márùn-ún láìsí oògùn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Self-resolving acute monoarthritis of the first metatarsophalangeal joint with spontaneous nocturnal onset and resolution within five days, the classic pattern of gouty arthritis requiring serum uric acid, joint aspiration for crystals, and dietary modification.

Doctor_English: Have you noticed any changes in your sense of smell or taste recently?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní oṣù mẹ́ta, òórùn àti ìtọ́ oúnjẹ ti yí padà fún mi. Oúnjẹ tó máa ń dára jù ni mo máa ń jẹ kò ní ìtọ́ mọ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Anosmia and dysgeusia of three months duration, which may indicate post-viral olfactory nerve damage, anterior cranial fossa pathology, or Parkinson's disease in which hyposmia frequently precedes motor symptoms by years.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling excessively worried about multiple things and finding it difficult to control the worry?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń yọ̀ nípa ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ nǹkan ní ìgbà-ìgbà — iṣẹ́, àwọn ọmọ, owó. Mi ò lè da ara mi dúró láti rò wọ́n.
Clinical_Translation_English: Generalised, uncontrollable worry across multiple life domains including occupational, parental, and financial concerns, consistent with generalised anxiety disorder requiring cognitive-behavioural therapy and pharmacological consideration.

Doctor_English: Have you had any difficulty hearing in noisy environments, or do people have to repeat themselves often?
Patient_Yoruba: Ní àyíká tó ń ariwo, mi ò máa ń gbọ́ ọ̀rọ̀ dáradára. Àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi máa ń tún ọ̀rọ̀ sọ fún mi ní ìgbà-ìgbà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Difficulty hearing speech in noise with frequent requests for repetition, suggesting mild to moderate sensorineural hearing loss, possibly age-related or noise-induced, requiring pure tone audiometry for quantification and hearing aid assessment.

Doctor_English: Is the child still feeding well, or have they lost interest in food and the breast?
Patient_Yoruba: Ọmọ mi ti kọ jíjẹun pátápátá. Ó kọ ọmu àti omi àgbádó. Ó ti jẹ́ bẹ́ẹ̀ fún ọjọ́ méjì.
Clinical_Translation_English: Complete feeding refusal including breast milk and complementary feeds for two days in a young child, indicating significant illness severity and risk of dehydration requiring urgent clinical assessment.

Doctor_English: With the urinary symptoms, have you had any loin pain, fever, or shaking chills?
Patient_Yoruba: Pẹ̀lú àìlera ito mi, irora wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ ẹ̀hin mi ọ̀tún, ibà, àti ìgbọn. Ó bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní àárọ̀ àná.
Clinical_Translation_English: Loin pain with fever and rigors accompanying urinary symptoms, the classic presentation of acute pyelonephritis, requiring blood and urine cultures before commencing intravenous antibiotics and assessing for urological complication.

Doctor_English: Can you point to exactly where the pain is — does it stay in one place or spread?
Patient_Yoruba: Irora náà wà níbí, ní apá ọ̀tún ikùn mi. Kò ń rìn, ó máa ń dúró síbẹ̀ náà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Pain is localised to the right iliac fossa with no radiation, consistent with appendiceal or ovarian pathology.

Doctor_English: Have you been having irregular periods since stopping the contraceptive pill, and for how long?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo dáwọ̀ lo ìṣọ́ ìdásí oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn. Oṣù mi kò padà rárá. Mi ò tíì lóyún àti mi ò ní àìsàn mìíràn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-pill amenorrhoea for six months exceeding the expected three-month recovery period, requiring exclusion of premature ovarian insufficiency, hyperprolactinaemia, hypothyroidism, and pregnancy before attributing to post-hormonal suppression.

Doctor_English: Has the patient been able to move all four limbs since the accident?
Patient_Yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìjàǹbá náà, ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀ méjì kò lè gbe. Ó ń fẹ̀sẹ̀ mú àti sọ pé kò ní ìmọ̀ ní apá ìsàlẹ̀ ìdí rẹ̀.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-traumatic bilateral lower limb paralysis with loss of sensation below the waist, indicating possible spinal cord injury at the thoracolumbar junction requiring immediate immobilisation and imaging.

Doctor_English: Has anyone witnessed you having a fit or convulsion, with shaking of your arms and legs?
Patient_Yoruba: Ìyàwó mi rí mi ń gbọn gbogbo ara, apá àti ẹsẹ̀ mi. Ó sọ pé ó pẹ́ fún ìṣẹ́jú marun. Kò mọ̀ mi ní ìgbà yẹn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Witnessed generalised tonic-clonic seizure lasting approximately five minutes with post-ictal unresponsiveness, constituting a first presentation of seizure requiring urgent neurological evaluation and electroencephalography.

Doctor_English: Has there been any flooding in your area recently, and have you had contact with floodwater?
Patient_Yoruba: Omi ìkún-omi bò abúlé wa ní ọ̀sẹ̀ méjì sẹ́yìn. Mo rìn nínú omi náà fún ọjọ́ mẹ́ta nítorí iṣẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Prolonged wading through floodwater for three days in a post-flood community setting, creating significant exposure risk for leptospirosis, wound infections, and waterborne gastrointestinal illness.

Doctor_English: Have you had any operations on your eyes such as cataract surgery or laser treatment?
Patient_Yoruba: A ṣe iṣẹ́ abẹ ojú fún cataracts ní ojú méjèéjì ní ọdún méjì sẹ́yìn. Ṣùgbọ́n ojú mi bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí rọ lẹ́ẹ̀kan sí i ní oṣù mẹ́fà sẹ́yìn.
Clinical_Translation_English: Post-cataract surgery visual deterioration raising concern for posterior capsule opacification or raised intraocular pressure requiring prompt ophthalmological review.

Doctor_English: Have you been feeling very unhappy about the way your body looks, and has this affected your eating?
Patient_Yoruba: Mi ò fẹ́ ara mi. Mo rí ara mi bí ẹni tó pọ̀ jù bí mo tilẹ̀ tẹ̀nìí. Mo ti dín jíjẹ mi kù láti gbìyànjú láti yí ara mi padà.
Clinical_Translation_English: Body dysmorphic cognitions with restriction of food intake in an adolescent who is objectively underweight but perceives themselves as overweight, indicating early anorexia nervosa requiring urgent eating disorder assessment and family-based therapy.

Doctor_English: Are you finding it difficult to remember recent events, or are you forgetting things more than usual?
Patient_Yoruba: Mo máa ń gbàgbé àwọn nǹkan tí mo ṣe ní ọjọ́ kéyìí. Mo sì ti gbàgbé orúkọ àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ mi tí mo ti mọ̀ tipẹ́tipẹ́.
Clinical_Translation_English: Progressive short-term memory impairment with emerging long-term memory involvement, indicating significant cognitive decline consistent with early to moderate Alzheimer's disease or vascular dementia.
